perturbation.original_text: For Product produced at Jasper facilities, Jasper shall invoice Premier on the date Jasper issues a Certificate of Analysis (COA) for those Products. Payment terms for these Product invoices shall be [***]. For Product produced at Heritage facilities, Heritage shall invoice Premier on the date the Products are loaded onto Premier's carrier. Payment terms for all these Product invoices shall be [***]. Failure by Premier to meet payment terms of any invoice shall result in interest being imposed on any unpaid balance at the rate of [***] per month, pro rata on a daily basis for partial months, accrued from its due date or in the event such rate exceeds that permitted to be charged by law, the maximum rate permitted by law.
perturbation.changed_text: For Product produced at Jasper facilities, Jasper shall invoice Premier on the date Jasper issues a Certificate of Analysis (COA) for those Products. Payment terms for these Product invoices shall be [***]. For Product produced at Heritage facilities, Heritage shall invoice Premier on the date the Products are loaded onto Premier's carrier. Payment terms for all these Product invoices shall be [***]. Failure by Premier to meet payment terms of any invoice shall result in interest being imposed on any unpaid balance at the rate of [***] per month, pro rata on a daily basis for partial months, accrued from its due date, but in no event shall interest be charged for the first 30 days of non-payment.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction in the payment terms. The original clause states that interest is imposed on any unpaid balance from its due date. The modified clause adds an exception stating that no interest will be charged for the first 30 days of non-payment. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding when the interest charges begin, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes over contractual obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For Product produced at Jasper facilities, Jasper shall invoice Premier on the date Jasper issues a Certificate of Analysis (COA) for those Products. Payment terms for these Product invoices shall be [***]. For Product produced at Heritage facilities, Heritage shall invoice Premier on the date the Products are loaded onto Premier's carrier. Payment terms for all these Product invoices shall be [***]. Failure by Premier to meet payment terms of any invoice shall result in interest being imposed on any unpaid balance at the rate of [***] per month, pro rata on a daily basis for partial months, accrued from its due date, but in no event shall interest be charged for the first 30 days of non-payment.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that interest is imposed on any unpaid balance from its due date. The changed clause adds an exception stating that no interest will be charged for the first 30 days of non-payment. This creates an in-text contradiction regarding when the interest charges begin, leading to uncertainty and potential disputes over contractual obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Heritage shall notify Premier via email within [***] that Products are available for shipment.
perturbation.changed_text: Heritage shall notify Premier via email within [***] that Products are available for shipment. Premier shall then have 24 hours to arrange for shipment.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds a requirement for Premier to arrange shipment within 24 hours of notification. While seemingly minor, it contradicts the original clause which only specified Heritage's notification duty. It creates ambiguity regarding whose responsibility it is to initiate and coordinate the logistics after notification, and what happens if Premier fails to arrange shipment within 24 hours. This can affect liability for delays or storage fees, creating an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the term of this Agreement, Heritage agrees to handle and store reasonable amounts of raw materials based upon the level of production expected [***]. With regard to finished Products, Heritage agrees during the Term to store finished Products at no cost to Premier for a period not to exceed [***] from the date of Heritage's issuance of a Certificate of Analysis ("COA"). Commencing on [***] after the date the COA is delivered to Premier, a warehouse fee will be imposed that will equal $[***], until such Products are delivered to Premier's carrier. [***] Capability of a corrugated shipping case to withstand double stacking shall be mutually determined and agreed by both Parties.
perturbation.justification: The original text only places the obligation to notify on Heritage. The changed text adds an obligation for Premier to arrange shipment within 24 hours of notification. The contradicted text states that Heritage agrees to store finished Products at no cost to Premier for a period not to exceed [***] from the date of Heritage's issuance of a Certificate of Analysis and imposes a warehouse fee commencing on [***] after the date the COA is delivered to Premier, until such Products are delivered to Premier's carrier. The added requirement for Premier to arrange shipment within 24 hours introduces ambiguity and potentially contradicts Heritage's storage obligation. If Premier fails to arrange shipment within 24 hours, it's unclear whether Heritage's storage obligation remains or if Premier becomes liable for warehouse fees earlier than anticipated in the original agreement, creating an inconsistency as defined in Perturbation Definition 5.

perturbation.original_text: Premier shall have the sole right, exercisable in its discretion, to initiate and direct the content and scope of a recall, market withdrawal, stock recovery, product correction and/or advisory safety communication (any one or more referred to as a "Recall Action") regarding the Products. At Premier's option, Premier can direct Heritage to, and upon such direction Heritage shall, conduct such Recall Action (and Heritage shall ensure Jasper's cooperation).
perturbation.changed_text: Premier shall have the sole right, exercisable in its discretion, to initiate and direct the content and scope of a recall, market withdrawal, stock recovery, product correction and/or advisory safety communication (any one or more referred to as a "Recall Action") regarding the Products. Heritage, however, shall have the right to refuse to conduct such Recall Action if Heritage believes the recall is not justified. At Premier's option, Premier can direct Heritage to, and upon such direction Heritage shall, conduct such Recall Action (and Heritage shall ensure Jasper's cooperation).
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the authority over recall actions. Originally, Premier has the sole right to initiate and direct recalls, and Heritage *shall* conduct the recall if directed. The modified clause now allows Heritage to refuse a recall if it deems it unjustified. This contradicts the initial statement of Premier's *sole* right and creates uncertainty about which party ultimately controls recall decisions and the legal ramifications of refusing a recall order.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Premier shall have the sole right, exercisable in its discretion, to initiate and direct the content and scope of a recall, market withdrawal, stock recovery, product correction and/or advisory safety communication (any one or more referred to as a "Recall Action") regarding the Products. Heritage, however, shall have the right to refuse to conduct such Recall Action if Heritage believes the recall is not justified. At Premier's option, Premier can direct Heritage to, and upon such direction Heritage shall, conduct such Recall Action (and Heritage shall ensure Jasper's cooperation). Premier shall determine, in its sole discretion, the manner, text and timing of any publicity to be given such matters upon prior consultation with Heritage. In the event a Recall Action is initiated or directed by Premier, Heritage agrees to fully cooperate and take all such steps as are reasonably requested to implement the Recall Action in a timely and complete manner. Any and all action to be taken in connection with a Recall Action shall be in accordance with FDA policies and other applicable laws and regulations. Heritage shall bear all costs, fees and out-of-pocket expenses associated with any Recall Action which results from (i) Heritage's or Jasper's negligence or willful misconduct, (ii) Heritage's or Jasper's failure to comply with Product Specifications or the Post Holdings Quality Expectations Manual set forth on Schedule A, (iii) any breach of this Agreement by Heritage or (iv) ingredients or packaging materials purchased by Heritage or Jasper. In all other cases, Premier shall bear all costs associated with any Recall Action.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. The original text and contradicted text state that Premier has the sole right to initiate and direct recalls, implying Heritage must comply. The modification allows Heritage to refuse a recall if it deems it unjustified, which directly contradicts Premier's 'sole right' and the obligation for Heritage to 'fully cooperate and take all such steps as are reasonably requested to implement the Recall Action'. This creates a significant inconsistency regarding the control and execution of recall actions, making the contract ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.
